mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of consumer credit and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment, rising industrial production reflects strong pricing power and margin expansion, driving positive short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with industrial production growth, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in at least two consecutive calendar years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative or flat in more than 65% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds 3.0%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combined effect of rising consumer price inflation and a lagging federal funds rate creates negative real interest rates, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, providing a positive drift for short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive relationship with payroll growth, or the signal underperforms the market in more than 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained elevation in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals a labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods where unemployment exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.
